turnaround_reason: Acadia is the largest standalone behavioral health provider in the US, beaten down from $30+ highs by debt concerns, negative FCF, and operationalExecution issues. The stock screened as 'recovering' — above its 200d MA and 108% of 100d MA, up 7.5% in 5 days. Q1 2026 earnings beat (EPS $0.37 vs $0.26 estimate, +39.7% surprise) and the company raised full-year 2026 Adjusted EBITDA and EPS guidance, signaling the turnaround is gaining traction.
bullish_points: Largest standalone behavioral health provider in the US — secular tailwind from rising mental health demand and policy support
Q1 2026 EPS beat by 39.7% ($0.37 vs $0.26), raised full-year guidance
Previously beat by 593% in Q4 2025 ($0.07 vs $0.01 estimate) — earnings inflection underway
14 analysts covering with mean target $28.86 vs current $25.17 — ~15% upside to mean
Revenue growth 7.6% TTM, gross margins 41.8%, operating margins 10.1% — operational business is profitable at the EBITDA line
Forward PE of 14.4x with EV/EBITDA of 8.6x — reasonable valuation for a turnaround with momentum
bearish_points: Negative profit margins (-32.8%) — company is net-loss at the bottom line due to high interest expense and possibly impairment charges
Negative free cash flow (-$217M) — cash burn is a real concern
High leverage: debt-to-equity 124%, total debt $2.69B vs total cash $158M
Short interest 38.6% of float — significant bearish positioning despite recent beat
No insider buying in past 6 months — only stock award grants to directors, no open-market purchases
yfinance news fields all null — limited recent news visibility from the data pull
catalyst: Q2 2026 earnings report on July 29, 2026 — continued EPS beat + guidance maintenance/raise would validate the turnaround trajectory and likely force short covering given 38.6% short interest
suggested_trade: Jan 2027 $25 calls or call spread $25/$35 — leveraged exposure to the July earnings catalyst with runway through Q3 reporting; the 38.6% short interest creates squeeze potential on a beat
key_risk: Negative free cash flow and $2.69B debt load — if earnings momentum stalls or guidance is cut, the high leverage creates a vicious downside cycle and shorts get vindicated
would_skip_if: Q2 earnings miss or guidance cut; FCF deterioration accelerates; debt covenant concerns emerge
